concept.conceptName: pH | Blood central venous | Chemistry - non-challenge
pH | Blood mixed venous | Chemistry - non-challenge

en.name: pH in venous blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Venous pH is the negative log of the hydrogen ion activity in venous blood, typically slightly lower than arterial pH (acidic shift from tissue CO2 production) [1]. Although arterial pH remains the reference for acid-base management, venous pH is widely used as a less invasive surrogate in the emergency department for the initial evaluation of diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA), where it shows close agreement with arterial pH (mean arterio-venous difference ~ 0.03 pH units) [2]. In low-flow states (shock, hypoperfusion) and respiratory failure, the gradient widens and venous pH may underestimate the degree of acidaemia or fail to reflect the respiratory component; arterial sampling remains preferable in those settings [2]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a blood gas analyser on a venous sample. pH is dimensionless. **Note: venous specimens.** This concept set covers peripheral venous, central venous (central venous catheter), mixed venous (pulmonary artery catheter) and capillary blood. The user should preserve the specimen distinction in analyses where the source-of-blood matters. **Note: temperature-corrected variant.** Modern blood gas analysers measure at 37 °C. A separate LOINC code exists for pH corrected to the patient's actual temperature (relevant in hypothermia). Both forms are included; downstream analyses that need to distinguish them must use the LOINC code, not the value. ## Included Concepts The set lists eight LOINC "Lab Test" concepts for pH in non-arterial blood (cord blood excluded), grouped by specimen. ### Venous blood - *3012544 / 2746-6 — pH of Venous blood (LOINC)* - *3038999 / 39486-6 — pH of Venous blood adjusted to patient's actual temperature (LOINC)* ### Mixed venous blood - *3012388 / 19213-8 — pH of Mixed venous blood (LOINC)* - *3030238 / 50980-2 — pH of Mixed venous blood adjusted to patient's actual temperature (LOINC)* ### Central venous blood - *1616438 / 97536-7 — pH of Central venous blood (LOINC)* - *1617447 / 97537-5 — pH of Central venous blood adjusted to patient's actual temperature (LOINC)* ### Capillary blood - *3009343 / 2745-8 — pH of Capillary blood (LOINC)* - *3040135 / 39485-8 — pH of Capillary blood adjusted to patient's actual temperature (LOINC)* **Note on units.** pH is dimensionless. LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS` is **`[pH]`** (concept 8482 — pH unit). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The set deliberately omits: - **Arterial blood specimens**: covered by the separate "pH in arterial blood" concept set. - **Cord blood specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. - **Generic blood (`pH of Blood`)**: ambiguous specimen, not used in routine clinical practice; out of scope. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports a venous pH without further specification: `3012544 / 2746-6 — pH of Venous blood`. - **Source explicitly mentions central venous or mixed venous**: map to the matching LOINC code rather than the generic venous one. - **Source reports pH from arterial blood**: do **not** map. Use "pH in arterial blood" instead. - **Do not confuse with**: arterial pH (separate concept set), urinary pH, gastric pH. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2746-6 — pH of Venous blood. Available: https://loinc.org/2746-6/ [2] Brandenburg MA, Dire DJ. Comparison of arterial and venous blood gas values in the initial emergency department evaluation of patients with diabetic ketoacidosis. *Ann Emerg Med*. 1998;31(4):459-65. Available: https://doi.org/10.1016/S0196-0644(98)70254-9
